summary: I was born and raised in Saint Paul, Minnesota, where much of my childhood unfolded. My family lived near the old Schmidt Brewery on the West End, and later we moved around the west side of Saint Paul. After my parents separated, I stayed in Saint Paul with my grandmother while my mom eventually moved back to be closer to me and my sister. Growing up in Saint Paul shaped much of my early life and family dynamics.

As a child, I lived near the old Schmidt Brewery in Saint Paul, Minnesota, specifically on the West End. It was a notable landmark close to where my family and I resided with my parents and grandfather. The brewery was part of the neighborhood environment that surrounded my early years before my parents separated.

We lived across the street from Monroe Elementary School when I was very young, near my grandfather's house in Saint Paul. Although I don't mention attending it directly, the school was a familiar part of my neighborhood and childhood environment. It was part of the community where my family lived before my parents separated.

When I was three years old, after my parents split up, my mom moved back to her hometown of Grand Forks, North Dakota. This move separated me and my sister, as we stayed behind with our grandmother in Saint Paul. My mom's return to Grand Forks marked a significant change in our family dynamics and living arrangements during my early childhood.

After my parents separated, my dad moved to a townhome in Apple Valley, Minnesota. Meanwhile, my sister and I stayed in Saint Paul with our grandmother. Apple Valley represented where my dad started his new life apart from my mom and us, marking a physical and emotional distance within our family during that time.
